title: lathyrus palustris var. pilosus
intro: Lathyrus palustris var. pilosus, also known as Marsh Pea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. Traditionally, the Marsh Pea has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, including coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate congestion and bronchitis, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of Lathyrus palustris var. pilosus typically involve infusions of the plant's leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and remedies.
